old_claim.note: Neuroscience provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from Artificial Neurons to Neuroscience; earliest mathematical neuron models are documented independent of modern neuroscience institutionalization.

source_shape.source_locator: 1943 paper introducing artificial neuron formalism (McCulloch & Pitts).
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source shows the mathematical neuron concept prior to later neuroscience scope used in this node's dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The citation dates the core modeled concept to 1943 and does not require the later neuroscience node as a hard prerequisite.

ontology_before: Artificial Neurons depended on Neuroscience as a hard/enabling prerequisite and were constrained by a later firstKnownDate.
ontology_after: Artificial Neurons now rests on mathematical and cybernetic foundations while allowing neuroscience as contextual downstream usage where relevant.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: remove unsupported hard prerequisite. Preserved: core early AI lineage via Mathematics and Cybernetics.
would_reject_if: A source demonstrated that modern neuroscience concepts represented by the scoped node are required before 1948 artificial-neuron models could be formulated.
short_reason: Core artificial-neuron models are mathematical and predate the scoped neuroscience node; this edge was creating a false chronology dependency.
